results.comment_text:
@user1:
Is this entirely correct? This phrasing implies that reducing the u-error by decreasing the u-resolution comes at the cost of the number of intervals, but they in fact increase, which seems to be a positive factor in reducing the u-error. 

I think you may have meant that 
```suggestion
Note that this comes at the cost of computation time as a result of the increased setup time and number of intervals. 
```

@author:
> Is this entirely correct? This phrasing implies that reducing the u-error by decreasing the u-resolution comes at the cost of the number of intervals, but they in fact increase, which seems to be a positive factor in reducing the u-error.

Ah, yes. This makes sense.

> I think you may have meant that

Yes, that's exactly what I meant!

@author:
Changed it this way, thanks!
results.test_code:
# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
import re

def test_sampling_hinv_cost_phrase_mentions_computation_time_not_intervals_cost__a1():
    source_path = '/workspace/doc/source/tutorial/stats/sampling_hinv.rst'
    source = open(source_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    expected = 'Note that this comes at the cost of computation time as a result of the increased\nsetup time and number of intervals.'
    assert expected in source, 'Documentation should clarify that decreasing u-resolution increases intervals and thus the cost is computation time (due to increased setup time and number of intervals). Expected the updated two-line sentence to be present.'

def test_sampling_hinv_cost_phrase_mentions_computation_time_not_intervals_cost__a2():
    source_path = '/workspace/doc/source/tutorial/stats/sampling_hinv.rst'
    source = open(source_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    expected = 'Note that this comes at the cost of computation time as a result of the increased\nsetup time and number of intervals.'
    old = 'Note that this comes at the cost of increased setup time and number of intervals.'
    assert old not in source, "Old wording still present; it incorrectly frames the increased number of intervals as the 'cost' rather than attributing cost to computation time."

def test_sampling_hinv_cost_phrase_mentions_computation_time_not_intervals_cost__a3():
    source_path = '/workspace/doc/source/tutorial/stats/sampling_hinv.rst'
    source = open(source_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    expected = 'Note that this comes at the cost of computation time as a result of the increased\nsetup time and number of intervals.'
    old = 'Note that this comes at the cost of increased setup time and number of intervals.'
    assert not re.search('Note that this comes at the cost of .*number of intervals\\.\\s*$', source, flags=re.MULTILINE) or expected in source, "Found a 'cost of ... number of intervals' sentence that does not match the clarified wording."

setup time and number of intervals.' in '.. _sampling-hinv:

Hermite interpolation based INVersion of CDF (HINV)
===================================================

.. currentmodule:: scipy.stats

* Required: CDF
* Optional: PDF, dPDF
* Speed:

  * Set-up: (very) slow
  * Sampling: (very) fast

HINV is a variant of numerical inversion, where the inverse CDF is approximated using
Hermite interpolation, i.e., the interval [0,1] is split into several intervals and
in each interval the inverse CDF is approximated by polynomials constructed by means
of values of the CDF and PDF at interval boundaries. This makes it possible to improve
the accuracy by splitting a particular interval without recomputations in unaffected
intervals. Three types of splines are implemented: linear, cubic, and quintic
interpolation. For linear interpolation only the CDF is required. Cubic interpolation
also requires PDF and quintic interpolation PDF and its derivative.

These splines have to be computed in a setup step. However, it only works for
distributions with bounded domain; for distributions with unbounded domain the tails
are chopped off such that the probability for the tail regions is small compared to
the give...g the u-resolution (maximum allowed u-error):

    >>> rng2 = NumericalInverseHermite(dist, u_resolution=1.e-13)
    >>> rng2.u_error(sample_size=1000000)
    UError(max_error=9.32027892364129e-14, mean_absolute_error=1.5194172675685075e-14)

Note that this comes at the cost of increased setup time and number of intervals.

    >>> rng1.intervals
    1022
    >>> rng2.intervals
    5687
    >>> from timeit import timeit
    >>> f = lambda: NumericalInverseHermite(dist, u_resolution=1e-10)
    >>> timeit(f, number=1)
    0.017409582000254886  # may vary
    >>> f = lambda: NumericalInverseHermite(dist, u_resolution=1e-13)
    >>> timeit(f, number=1)
    0.08671202100003939  # may vary

Please see [1]_ and [2]_ for more details on this method.

References
----------

.. [1] Hörmann, Wolfgang, and Josef Leydold. "Continuous random variate
       generation by fast numerical inversion." ACM Transactions on
       Modeling and Computer Simulation (TOMACS) 13.4 (2003): 347-362.
.. [2] UNU.RAN reference manual, Section 5.3.5,
       "HINV - Hermite interpolation based INVersion of CDF",
       https://statmath.wu.ac.at/software/unuran/doc/unuran.html#HINV
'

def test_sampling_hinv_uses_consistent_scientific_notation_for_u_resolution__a1():
    path = '/workspace/doc/source/tutorial/stats/sampling_hinv.rst'

    assert re.search('u_resolution\\s*=\\s*1e-10\\b', source), "Expected sampling_hinv.rst to contain 'u_resolution=1e-10' after the change."

import re

def test_hinv_docstring_uses_correct_corresponding_in_u_error_sentence__a1():
    """
    Source-level regression test for the reviewed docstring change:
    in the HINV docstring's u-error sentence, "corresponding" should be spelled
    correctly in the specific phrase.

    The repository may still contain the old misspelling elsewhere (e.g. in
    other docstrings), so we only test the relevant sentence fragment.
    """
    path = '/workspace/scipy/stats/_unuran/unuran_wrapper.pyx.templ'
    source = open(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    hinv_pos = source.find('cdef class NumericalInverseHermite(Method):')
    assert hinv_pos != -1, 'Sanity check: NumericalInverseHermite definition not found.'

def test_hinv_docstring_uses_correct_corresponding_in_u_error_sentence__a2():
    """
    Source-level regression test for the reviewed docstring change:
    in the HINV docstring's u-error sentence, "corresponding" should be spelled
    correctly in the specific phrase.

    The repository may still contain the old misspelling elsewhere (e.g. in
    other docstrings), so we only test the relevant sentence fragment.
    """
    path = '/workspace/scipy/stats/_unuran/unuran_wrapper.pyx.templ'
    source = open(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    hinv_pos = source.find('cdef class NumericalInverseHermite(Method):')
    window = source[hinv_pos:hinv_pos + 12000]
    assert 'maximal numerical error in "u-direction"' in window, 'Sanity check: expected HINV docstring u-error description not found.'

def test_hinv_docstring_uses_correct_corresponding_in_u_error_sentence__a3():
    """
    Source-level regression test for the reviewed docstring change:
    in the HINV docstring's u-error sentence, "corresponding" should be spelled
    correctly in the specific phrase.

    The repository may still contain the old misspelling elsewhere (e.g. in
    other docstrings), so we only test the relevant sentence fragment.
    """
    path = '/workspace/scipy/stats/_unuran/unuran_wrapper.pyx.templ'
    source = open(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    hinv_pos = source.find('cdef class NumericalInverseHermite(Method):')
    window = source[hinv_pos:hinv_pos + 12000]
    assert 'approximate percentile corresponding to the' in window, "Expected HINV docstring to contain the corrected phrase 'approximate percentile corresponding to the' in the u-error description."

def test_hinv_docstring_uses_correct_corresponding_in_u_error_sentence__a4():
    """
    Source-level regression test for the reviewed docstring change:
    in the HINV docstring's u-error sentence, "corresponding" should be spelled
    correctly in the specific phrase.

    The repository may still contain the old misspelling elsewhere (e.g. in
    other docstrings), so we only test the relevant sentence fragment.
    """
    path = '/workspace/scipy/stats/_unuran/unuran_wrapper.pyx.templ'
    source = open(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    hinv_pos = source.find('cdef class NumericalInverseHermite(Method):')
    window = source[hinv_pos:hinv_pos + 12000]
    assert re.search('``\\|U\\s*-\\s*CDF\\(X\\)\\|``', window), 'Expected the u-error math expression ``|U - CDF(X)|`` in the HINV docstring.'

results.comment_text:
@user1:
```suggestion
``|U - CDF(X)|`` where ``X`` is the approximate percentile corresponding to the
```

This is misspelled in the API doc as well

@author:
Corrected both here and in API docs.

# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level

import re

def test_sampling_hinv_typo_corrected_corresponding__a1():
    path = '/workspace/doc/source/tutorial/stats/sampling_hinv.rst'
    source = open(path, encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'corressponding' not in source, f"Found misspelling 'corressponding' in {path}; expected it to be corrected to 'corresponding'."

def test_sampling_hinv_typo_corrected_corresponding__a2():
    path = '/workspace/doc/source/tutorial/stats/sampling_hinv.rst'
    source = open(path, encoding='utf-8').read()
    pattern = 'u-direction.*?\\|U\\s*-\\s*CDF\\(X\\)\\|.*?approximate percentile corresponding'
    assert re.search(pattern, source, flags=re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL), f"Did not find the corrected phrasing with 'approximate percentile corresponding' near the u-direction error formula in {path}."

results.comment_text:
@user1:
```suggestion
Higher orders result in a fewer number of intervals:
```

very nitpicky suggestion but "result" is an intransitive verb in this usage 

@author:
Done.
results.test_code:
# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
import re

def test_sampling_hinv_grammar_fix_higher_orders_sentence__a1():
    source = open('/workspace/doc/source/tutorial/stats/sampling_hinv.rst', encoding='utf-8').read()
    bad = 'Higher orders result is fewer number of intervals:'
    good = 'Higher orders result in a fewer number of intervals:'
    assert bad not in source, f'The documentation should not contain the ungrammatical sentence {bad!r}; it should be replaced with {good!r}.'

def test_sampling_hinv_grammar_fix_higher_orders_sentence__a2():
    source = open('/workspace/doc/source/tutorial/stats/sampling_hinv.rst', encoding='utf-8').read()
    bad = 'Higher orders result is fewer number of intervals:'
    good = 'Higher orders result in a fewer number of intervals:'
    assert good in source, f'The documentation should contain the corrected sentence {good!r} (as per the review comment fix).'

def test_sampling_hinv_grammar_fix_higher_orders_sentence__a3():
    source = open('/workspace/doc/source/tutorial/stats/sampling_hinv.rst', encoding='utf-8').read()
    bad = 'Higher orders result is fewer number of intervals:'
    good = 'Higher orders result in a fewer number of intervals:'
    assert len(re.findall(re.escape(good), source)) == 1, 'Expected the corrected sentence to appear exactly once in the document.'
